summary: iter-148 E2E verification of AdminHealth page + Decimate tooltip estimators. Backend admin_health pytest suite (12/12) re-run and green. All 5 requested frontend flows verified against the live preview URL using the pre-seeded admin session.

test_results.Test1_backend_admin_endpoints: PASS — 12/12 pytest (tests/test_admin_health.py) + curl spot-check of GET /api/admin/gallery-stats returns gallery+components with total, featured, by_category, missing_thumbnail(33/78), missing_stl, oversized_stl, orphaned_no_owner, public, private, oldest, newest.
test_results.Test2_admin_health_page: PASS — /admin/health renders with data-testid='admin-health-page'. Gallery card shows 46 total (public 35, private 11, missing_thumbnail 33, missing_stl 0, oversized 0, orphaned 0, by_category chips: misc 39, organizers 2, household 2, toys 1, tools 1, mechanical 1). Components card shows 84 total, missing_thumbnail 78, missing_stl 2, by_category (mechanical 72, misc 12). Regen button label reads 'Regenerate 111 missing'. Click → status transitions to 'done' with counts '111/111 processed · 0 regenerated · 109 skipped', progress bar animates, success toast 'Regenerated 0 thumbnails · 2 errors' fires. AI failure section renders with '0/0' 24h/7d stat cards and 'No recent AI generation failures. Nice.' message (data-testid='admin-health-ai-clean').
test_results.Test3_admin_health_link: PASS — data-testid='admin-health-link' visible in /admin header with text 'Health'. Clicking navigates to /admin/health and admin-health-page renders.
test_results.Test4_decimate_tooltip_estimators: PASS — Imported a real 1,280-tri icosphere STL via dispatched drop event. Opened Printability panel via data-testid='printability-open-btn'. Decimate row rendered (data-testid='printability-decimate-row'). Current summary shows 'Current: 1,280 tris (~63 KB · 60 fps)' via data-testid='printability-decimate-current-est'. Preset chips show at bottom: mini '1.2 MB · 60 fps', functional '586 KB · 60 fps', low_poly '146 KB · 60 fps' via data-testid='printability-decimate-{key}-est'. Hover title tooltip on each chip contains both 'Est. STL size:' and 'Est. viewport:' lines. Tooltip on current-line contains 'STL size:' and 'Viewport:'.
test_results.Test5_auth_gating: PASS — (a) non-admin authed user (freshly registered via /api/auth/register) navigating to /admin/health redirects to '/'. (b) anonymous user (cookies cleared) navigating to /admin/health redirects to '/'. admin-health-page is not visible in either case.

notes: The three preset chip estimates use 1024-base KB/MB formatting, so 12k tris renders as '586 KB' rather than the task's approximation of '600 KB'. This is expected (12000*50 = 600000 bytes = 585.9 KB). The heuristic 50 bytes/tri is applied exactly as specified; only the display base differs. Not a bug.

During Test 2 regen run, 0 items were successfully regenerated (2 errors + 109 skipped_no_stl). This reflects the underlying data state (previous regen already handled the renderable items; the remaining 33+78 rows have missing/unrenderable STL bodies) — not a bug in the endpoint. Backend regen worker returned status='done' cleanly and the errors <details> block rendered.

AdminHealth page reads /api/admin/gallery-stats (200) and /api/admin/ai-errors (200) on load; regen POST returns started=true and polling to /api/admin/regenerate-thumbnails/status works at 1.5s cadence. All four admin endpoints exercised end-to-end from the browser.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminHealth.jsx line 133: pluralization ternary for 'thumbnail(s)' and 'error(s)' — correct.
PrintabilityReportPanel.jsx line 524-534: stlBytes/fmtBytes/estFps helpers are pure and unit-testable; documented rationale for 50 bytes/tri + 30M/tri FPS heuristic is present in the comment block.
Both new pieces have solid data-testid coverage; no test-blocking gaps observed.
test_credentials: Admin session: session_token=st_admin_health_1784561381387 (already seeded). Fresh regular user registered inline during Test 5a via POST /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: None persisted. Test 4 briefly dropped /app/frontend/public/test_ico_iter148.stl for the drag-drop simulation; removed after the test completed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-148 features verified end-to-end. Backend admin_health suite pinned at 12/12. If retesting on a preview DB with fewer missing thumbnails, the regen job may complete in <2s and only the 'done' status will be observable (that's fine — the button/progress bar still work). For Test 4, dropping any small STL onto the workspace is enough; the row only appears when >=1 imported mesh is on the plate.
